title: PyTorch FFT Layer Tests crash with Windows fatal exception: access violation in torch.randn
signature: Windows fatal exception: access violation in torch.randn during FFT test _prepare_input|Flaky Test

failed_jobs.name: Pytorch Layer Tests / PyTorch Layer Tests
failed_jobs.symptom: Windows fatal exception: access violation in torch.randn during test_fft.py::TestFFT::test_2d

failed_jobs.name: ci/gha_overall_status_windows
failed_jobs.symptom: Overall status check failed due to dependent job failure

key_errors:
Windows fatal exception: access violation
  File pytorch_layer_test_class.py, line 57 in randn
  File test_fft.py, line 76 in _prepare_input
  File pytorch_layer_test_class.py, line 310 in _test_impl
  File test_fft.py, line 125 in test_2d
root_cause: A Windows access violation (equivalent of SIGSEGV) occurred inside torch.randn() while preparing inputs for test_fft.py::TestFFT::test_2d. The crash happens during memory allocation for a random tensor, which is unrelated to the PR's GPU GroupQueryAttention changes. This is characteristic of a transient Windows runner memory/heap corruption issue or a flaky test environment problem. The PR changes only affect GPU plugin ScatterUpdate/Slice primitives and a new MatcherPass for KV scale precision – none of which would cause torch.randn to crash on CPU FFT tests.
reproduction_steps: N/A – Windows runner memory corruption is not reproducibly triggered by code changes.
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed jobs to verify this is transient.
If the failure persists, check Windows runner health and memory availability.
If it recurs consistently for this test, investigate torch version compatibility with the Windows runner environment.
prevention_strategies: Monitor Windows runner health metrics. Consider adding retry logic for test runs that crash with access violations rather than assertion failures.
historical_context: No previous occurrences of this specific Windows access violation in torch.randn for FFT tests found in the investigation database.
